action.hypothesis: Now that gated holds the full frontier at K=128, K=64, and K=32, the next informative move is to test whether the low-K regime is over-capacitated rather than quality-limited. Reducing gated expansion_factor from 16 to 8 at K=32 may preserve most of the newly won quality while improving cost signals and possibly sharpening feature competition enough to strengthen the Pareto tradeoff.
action.summary: Run a proxy gated K=32 stabilization pass with AUXK_ALPHA=0.01 and EXPANSION_FACTOR=8 to test for a better low-K cost-quality tradeoff.
action.self_review: This keeps the search on the strongest validated family and changes only expansion factor, which is the cleanest next family-specific axis after gated swept the tested K values. The main risk is a quality drop from reduced capacity, but the K=32 regime is the most plausible place for over-capacity to be hurting efficiency rather than helping reconstruction.
notes_to_memory: The gated family now owns the full frontier across K=128, K=64, and K=32 under AUXK_ALPHA=0.01, so follow-up should shift from architecture replacement to family-specific tradeoff shaping.
This round probes whether the new gated K=32 point is over-capacitated at EXPANSION_FACTOR=16 and can be improved or made cheaper by reducing latent width.
Keeping architecture, optimizer, learning rate, AUXK_ALPHA, and K fixed isolates expansion factor as the only changed variable for a clean low-K cost-quality comparison.
next_hypotheses: If gated K=32 with EXPANSION_FACTOR=8 maintains similar quality with better runtime or memory, treat it as the preferred low-K Pareto point and consider the same capacity reduction at K=64.
If EXPANSION_FACTOR=8 improves K=32 proxy FVU outright, promote it to full and treat the earlier EF=16 setting as unnecessarily wide for the ultra-low-K regime.
If EXPANSION_FACTOR=8 degrades K=32 substantially, keep EF=16 as the low-K gated default and test a different family only if new architecture exploration becomes necessary again.
